metadata.task: Set glider_1 (object_1) moving in the +x direction at 2 m/s while glider_2 (object_2) starts at rest, then simulate their collision on the track. Report the final x velocities of object_1 and object_2 as two numbers rounded to the nearest tenth: v1_final, v2_final.
metadata.description: The two gliders have equal mass and start on a nearly frictionless track. Run the collision and read the velocities after contact has completed; do not answer with the conservation equation.

expected_behavior: The LLM sets the initial velocity, simulates the collision, and records both final x velocities.
reasoning: For an equal-mass head-on collision with the second glider initially at rest, the expected final x velocities are approximately 0.0 and 2.0.
